2604.04133.title: Learning Robust Visual Features in Computed Tomography Enables Efficient Transfer Learning for Clinical Tasks
2604.04133.paper_summary: There is substantial interest in developing artificial intelligence systems to support radiologists across tasks ranging from segmentation to report generation. Existing computed tomography (CT) foundation models have largely focused on building generalist vision-language systems capable of tasks such as question answering and report generation. However, training reliable vision-language systems requires paired image-text data at a scale that remains unavailable in CT. Moreover, adapting the underlying visual representations to downstream tasks typically requires partial or full backbone fine-tuning, a computationally demanding process inaccessible to many research groups. Instead, foundation models should prioritise learning robust visual representations that enable efficient transfer to new tasks with minimal labelled data and without backbone fine-tuning. We present VoxelFM, a 3D CT foundation model trained with self-distillation using the DINO framework, which learns semantically rich features without language supervision. We evaluated VoxelFM across seven categories of clinically relevant downstream tasks using frozen backbone representations with lightweight probes: classification, regression, survival analysis, instance retrieval, localisation, segmentation, and report generation. VoxelFM matched or outperformed four existing CT foundation models across all task categories. Despite receiving no language supervision during pre-training, VoxelFM surpassed models explicitly trained with language-alignment objectives, including on report generation. Our results indicate that current CT foundation models perform significantly better as feature extractors for lightweight probes rather than as vision encoders for vision-language models. Model weights and training code are publicly available.
2604.04133.paper_summary_zh: 對於開發人工智慧系統以支持放射科醫生在從分割到報告生成等任務方面的工作，存在著相當大的興趣。現有的計算機斷層掃描（CT）基礎模型主要集中於構建能夠執行問題回答和報告生成等任務的通用視覺-語言系統。然而，訓練可靠的視覺-語言系統需要配對的圖像-文本數據，這在CT中仍然無法獲得。此外，將基礎的視覺表示適應於下游任務通常需要部分或完全的主幹微調，這是一個計算需求高的過程，許多研究小組無法獲得。相反，基礎模型應優先學習穩健的視覺表示，以便在最少標記數據的情況下高效轉移到新任務，而無需主幹微調。我們提出了VoxelFM，一個使用DINO框架進行自我蒸餾訓練的3D CT基礎模型，該模型在沒有語言監督的情況下學習語義豐富的特徵。我們使用凍結的主幹表示和輕量級探針，對VoxelFM在七個與臨床相關的下游任務類別上進行了評估：分類、回歸、生存分析、實例檢索、定位、分割和報告生成。VoxelFM在所有任務類別中與四個現有的CT基礎模型相匹配或超越。儘管在預訓練期間未接受語言監督，VoxelFM在報告生成等任務上超越了明確以語言對齊目標進行訓練的模型。我們的結果表明，當前的CT基礎模型在作為輕量級探針的特徵提取器方面表現顯著優於作為視覺-語言模型的視覺編碼器。模型權重和訓練代碼已公開提供。
2604.04133.author: Rubén Moreno-Aguado et.al.
2604.04133.authors: Rubén Moreno-Aguado, Alba Magallón, Victor Moreno, Yingying Fang, Guang Yang
